items.title: Map safe in-image MCP verification boundaries
items.description:
Type: verification-boundary planning slice
Readiness: human-in-the-loop (HITL) for live image execution; documentation/static verification is autonomous agent-ready (AFK)

Goal: map what MCP-Pharo work can be verified without launching live images and what must wait for the future isolated runner boundary.

Acceptance criteria:
- The item lists safe static or metadata-level verification available without starting a live image.
- The item lists live-image checks that require disposable image inputs, cleanup hooks, and explicit approval.
- The boundary aligns with the DevNexus-Pharo and PLexus safety rules for no live runtime mutation in the current dogfood target.
- Follow-up work is component-owned and does not require chat memory to interpret.

Verification:
- Use read-only file/status inspection only.
- Do not launch images or mutate the local Pharo image state.

items.title: Run MCP-Pharo verification through isolated runner
items.description:
Type: live-image verification follow-up
Readiness: blocked on isolated PLexus runner and human approval
Source plan: PLAN.md priority candidate 9.

Goal: verify MCP-Pharo inside a disposable image through the approved PLexus runner.

Acceptance criteria:
- The approval names the source image or template, copied image name pattern, state root, workspace id, target id, and MCP port policy.
- The verification lists the exact tool-list, package/class/method, and test checks to run through routed pharo calls.
- Cleanup closes the image, stops owned processes, deletes copied images, and retains logs/artifacts.
- No direct live image mutation is performed outside the runner.

Blockers and boundaries:
- Blocked on approval and availability of the isolated runner harness.
- Do not launch images, save images, run image-side tests, or mutate Pharo runtime state outside the approved runner.

Verification:
- Run only after the isolated runner exists and approval is recorded.
- Preserve source worktree state and record final status.

Publication notes:
- MCP-Pharo component policy is review_handoff with push false unless a human explicitly approves a publication path.
- Runtime verification evidence should be retained with the runner artifacts and referenced from the work item comments.

items.title: Decide MCP-Pharo publication path for local develop commits
items.description:
Type: publication decision slice
Readiness: human-in-the-loop (HITL)
Source plan: PLAN.md priority candidate 1.

Current finding: MCP-Pharo `develop` is ahead of `origin/develop` by two local review-handoff commits: `0a38755` (`Document DevNexus verification boundary`) and `4c37fb0` (`Keep verification boundary project-local`).

Goal: decide whether the local MCP-Pharo commits remain local review handoff, are pushed to a review branch, or are published to `develop` after review.

Acceptance criteria:
- The decision records the chosen publication path: local review handoff, review branch, or direct develop publication.
- Required reviewer/owner approval is captured before any push to develop.
- If a review branch is chosen, branch name, remote, and retained verification evidence are recorded.
- The work item comment explains why the chosen path matches MCP-Pharo's review_handoff policy.

Blockers and boundaries:
- Blocked on human/reviewer publication decision.
- Do not push MCP-Pharo develop or mutate live Pharo images as part of this decision.

Verification:
- Before publication, inspect git status/log for `origin/develop...develop` and preserve unrelated local state.
- After a decision, run only approved static or runner-backed verification and record the result.

Publication notes:
- Component policy is review_handoff with push false unless the human explicitly approves a branch push or direct develop publication.

items.title: Simplify MCP-Pharo where predicate mode API
items.description:
Type: MCP query API simplification slice
Readiness: autonomous agent-ready for exported source changes; live/image test execution remains human-in-the-loop until an isolated runner is approved.
Source Vibe issue: FCD-53.

Current finding: MCP-Pharo still uses `filterMode` for top-level filtering and for `where` filter predicates, while `where` field predicates use `mode`. `AGENTS.md` still instructs agents to use `filterMode` for reference lookups. The Vibe issue is still relevant.

Goal: simplify the `where` predicate language so matching mode is expressed consistently, preferably with a single `mode` key inside `where` predicates including reference predicates.

Acceptance criteria:
- `MCPWhereSpec` parses `where` filter/reference predicates using `mode` consistently.
- Tool schemas and descriptions no longer require `where.filterMode`; remove backward compatibility if that keeps the implementation clearer.
- Tests cover field, filter, selectorReference, classReference, and variableReference predicates under the settled spelling.
- `AGENTS.md` and user docs show the updated query examples.

Blockers and boundaries:
- Do not run live image tests, save images, or mutate the active image outside an approved runner.

Verification:
- Static source/test inspection before runner approval.
- Run SmalltalkCI or image-side tests only through the approved isolated runner.

Publication notes:
- MCP-Pharo uses review_handoff publication unless a human approves a branch push or direct develop publication.

items.title: Document isolated local SmalltalkCI runner for MCP-Pharo
items.description:
Type: verification runner documentation slice
Readiness: autonomous agent-ready for documentation and static config review; human-in-the-loop for executing live/local SmalltalkCI.
Source Vibe issue: FCD-66.

Current finding: `.smalltalk.ston` exists and `docs/verification-boundary.md` describes static vs live verification, but there is no documented isolated local SmalltalkCI command, temporary image/profile/workspace boundary, cleanup sequence, or branch/config conflict decision.

Goal: document an automation-safe local SmalltalkCI runner or equivalent PLexus-managed runner path for MCP-Pharo.

Acceptance criteria:
- The docs name the supported Pharo versions, prerequisites, command shape, temporary image/profile/workspace boundary, and cleanup responsibilities.
- The docs explain what artifacts may be created and where logs/coverage are retained.
- The workflow does not save or mutate the user's active development image.
- The existing `.smalltalk.ston` branch/config situation is explicitly resolved or recorded as a blocker.

Blockers and boundaries:
- Do not execute live SmalltalkCI or image tests without explicit runner approval.

Verification:
- Static review of `.smalltalk.ston`, baseline groups, package names, and docs.
- Live runner validation only after approval.

Publication notes:
- MCP-Pharo uses review_handoff publication unless a human approves a branch push or direct develop publication.

local-1.body:
Completed in this cycle. Added docs/dev-nexus-verification-boundary.md and linked it from readme.md. The boundary lists safe static verification, explicitly blocks pharo MCP/live image use in planning-only cycles, lists checks requiring the isolated PLexus runner, and names disposable inputs required for future live verification. No pharo MCP tool, image launch, image save, image-side test run, or repository mutation was performed.

Verification passed: git diff --check; static grep of the boundary and BaselineOfMCP package/test declarations. Created local review-handoff commit 0a38755 on develop and blocked follow-up mcp-pharo:local-2 for future runner-backed live verification.

local-2.body: Updated during dev-nexus:local-5 plan split to include explicit source-plan, blocker, verification, and publication notes. Live runtime execution remains blocked until the isolated runner is approved.

Still blocked on the same isolated runner boundary as PLexus/DevNexus-Pharo live verification. Static repository checks are fine, but image-side verification should wait for an approved disposable runner and cleanup policy.

local-3.body: Publication decision completed on 2026-05-16: direct push to origin/develop was rejected because origin/develop had six newer commits. No force push or rebase was performed. The two local verification-boundary commits 0a38755 and 4c37fb0 were published instead as review branch origin/review/mcp-pharo-verification-boundary-20260516 for handoff. Pull request URL offered by GitHub: https://github.com/Evref-BL/MCP/pull/new/review/mcp-pharo-verification-boundary-20260516.

Final integration update on 2026-05-16: after fetching Mac-side origin/develop work, local develop merged origin/develop cleanly, preserving commits 0a38755 and 4c37fb0. Pushed merged develop to origin as 9d90fd8, verified origin/review/mcp-pharo-verification-boundary-20260516 was an ancestor of HEAD, then deleted the remote review branch.

local-4.body: Promoted for parallel non-live dogfood wave. Scope: source-level where predicate mode API simplification; no live image tests or image mutation.

Blocked after user correction: MCP-Pharo work must use direct Pharo MCP rather than file-first edits. No direct mcp__pharo__ tool namespace is available, PLexus reports no registered image routes, and no imageId is available for plexus_route_to_image. No files were changed and no live image was launched.

Planning update: MCP-Pharo source work remains blocked until DevNexus-Pharo plugin setup proves that subagents receive direct Pharo MCP access through scoped PLexus/gateway infrastructure. New prerequisite work items: dev-nexus:local-13, plexus:local-8, dev-nexus-pharo:local-4, and blocked live smoke dev-nexus-pharo:local-5.

local-5.body: Promoted for parallel non-live dogfood wave. Scope: isolated local SmalltalkCI runner documentation/static config review; no live SmalltalkCI execution.

Blocked after user correction: MCP-Pharo work must use direct Pharo MCP rather than file-first edits. No direct mcp__pharo__ tool namespace is available, PLexus reports no registered image routes, and no imageId is available for plexus_route_to_image. No files were changed and no live image was launched.

Planning update: MCP-Pharo source work remains blocked until DevNexus-Pharo plugin setup proves that subagents receive direct Pharo MCP access through scoped PLexus/gateway infrastructure. New prerequisite work items: dev-nexus:local-13, plexus:local-8, dev-nexus-pharo:local-4, and blocked live smoke dev-nexus-pharo:local-5.

Completed by upstream MCP-Pharo commit f84ae31 after pulling origin/main. The commit adds the repo-local pharo-ci-repro skill, Docker smalltalkCI helper, and log-reading reference under user/skills/pharo-ci-repro. Static review confirms the documented command shape uses scratch copied work trees and external logs without mutating the active Pharo image. No Docker or live image run was performed in this dogfood refresh because live services remain policy-gated.
